title: Aluminum Facing 'Very Large Supply Hole,' JPM Says
summary: J.P. Morgan analysts predict aluminum prices will rise toward $4,000 per metric ton due to a significant supply shortage exacerbated by Middle East conflicts. The base case suggests prices will stabilize around $3,500 per metric ton in the second half of 2026.

title: Aluminium Price Today
summary: Aluminum prices hit a four-year high at $3,199.00 per metric ton amid Middle East supply risks, with futures trading between $3,173.25 and $3,199.00. The market is reacting to geopolitical tensions affecting global supply chains.

title: Aluminium Prices Are On The Rise As US Premium At Record High
summary: Aluminum prices have surged 13% in 2025, with LME prices reaching three-year highs due to China's capacity restrictions and supply concerns over boxside grades. J.P. Morgan experts forecast prices could reach $3,000 per metric ton, driven by copper-aluminum replacement effects.

title: The Outlook For Aluminum, Steel & Copper Prices
summary: J.P. Morgan forecasts an average aluminum price of $2,200/mt for Q2 2025, with global demand growth reduced to 1% year-over-year, creating a surplus of 200 kmt in 2025. The outlook reflects adjusted demand estimates and market surplus projections.

title: Aluminum Prices Ease After Iran Shock as Trade Pressure Keeps Costs in Focus
summary: Aluminum prices eased after the Iran shock, with futures trading around $3,400 per ton, though some analysts predict a surge past $4,000. Trade pressures continue to influence costs amid geopolitical uncertainties.

title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
summary: A new aluminum smelter in Oklahoma with a capacity of about 600,000 tons per year is nearly doubling current US domestic production, as only six smelting operations remain in the US. The U.S. government must intervene to increase domestic aluminum production and reduce reliance on foreign suppliers, according to the Aluminum Association.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Century Aluminum has decided to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum, as reported by Fox News senior correspondent Steve Harrigan. This decision reflects the company's response to current market pressures and policy changes.

title: America's big new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal
summary: The companies building America's new aluminum smelter expect to start construction in late 2026 and begin producing metal by the end of the decade, but are still finalizing the power deal. This delay highlights the critical role of energy infrastructure in expanding U.S. aluminum capacity.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights Hydro's HalZero technology, which eliminates CO2 emissions during aluminum smelting by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop. It also covers innovations in removing metal impurities from recycled aluminum and Rio Tinto's use of hydrogen to replace natural gas in alumina refining.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that captures over 99% of aluminum ions from hazardous waste streams in aluminum production. The captured aluminum can be upcycled into bulk production, increasing yield while significantly reducing waste.

title: Aluminum Recycling: Innovation & Sustainability
summary: TOMRA explores how cutting-edge technologies like Dynamic LIBS and deep learning are transforming aluminum recycling by enhancing material purity and efficiency. These innovations are fueling the green revolution in the aluminum industry by making recycling more sustainable.

title: American Steel Just Got More Expensive. Buyers Blame Tariffs.
summary: Cleveland-Cliffs sold steel for an average price of $1,015 per ton in the second quarter, up from $980 in the first quarter, driven by tariffs on steel and aluminum imposed by President Trump. American manufacturers are passing these additional costs onto domestic producers in industries like automotive and military equipment, causing a recent surge in U.S. steel prices.

title: Steel prices, news & market analysis | Argus Media
summary: The UK government published an update to its steel safeguard quotas, increasing the overall quota volume to 3.218 million tons, which is 21% higher than provisional volumes. The EU HRC quota was revised up to 375,000 tons per year, while quotas for India, South Korea, and the residual category were also significantly raised.

title: US Steel Costs Impacted by War, Trade Deals
summary: Steel pricing is currently impacted by rising diesel prices, which have increased 50% due to the Iran war, and ongoing trade deals. The market is in a nearly nine-month march higher with prices near $1,100, and analysts predict it may reach $1,500 for hot-rolled coil before peaking in late spring or early summer.

title: US Midwest Domestic Hot-Rolled Coil Steel Futures Historical Data
summary: Over the past 12 months, US Steel Coil futures have changed by 30.92%, reflecting significant market volatility. Today's trading range for US Steel Coil futures is between 1,153.00 and 1,158.00.

title: Steel Price News: Are Prices About to Drop? Latest Hot Rolled Coil
summary: Latest steel prices for June 2026 show Hot-Rolled Coil at $1,109 per ton, Cold Rolled Steel at $1,293 per ton, and Coated Steel at $1,380 per ton. The article analyzes whether these prices are poised to drop in the coming months.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,064 CNY per ton on June 26, 2026, marking a 0.20% increase from the previous day. Although the price has fallen 2.95% over the past month, it remains 3.44% higher than the previous year.

title: We just got our revised steel prices for this month. It's bad
summary: Users report receiving revised steel pricing updates for the month, with prices up another 21.6% since March. The total price increase year-to-date is 58.8%, driven by major spikes in costs during recent months.

title: Hoa Phat Starts Up New Production Line From Primetals Technologies
source: Association for Iron & Steel Technology - AIST.org
summary: Hoa Phat Dung Quat Steel JSC has officially started up its new steel production line, developed with Primetals Technologies. This expansion marks a significant increase in the company's steel production capacity in Vietnam.

title: Hybar LLC Unveils Plans for Second Scrap Metal Recycling Steel Rebar Mill
source: Association for Iron & Steel Technology - AIST.org
summary: Hybar LLC announced plans to build a second scrap metal recycling steel rebar mill adjacent to its existing facility in Arkansas. The project is backed by a US$1.1 billion investment and aims to boost regional steel rebar production capacity.

title: SMU Survey: Steel Buyers' Sentiment Indices Stay Optimistic
summary: The latest Steel Market Update survey reveals that steel buyers remain optimistic about market conditions despite recent fluctuations. This positive sentiment suggests stable demand for steel production and capacity in the coming months.

title: Hybar Expansion Accelerates with New Rebar Mill Project
summary: Hybar's expansion plans are accelerating as the company moves forward with its new rebar mill project in Arkansas. The initiative is expected to significantly enhance the company's steel production capabilities and market presence.

title: Copper up 9% since Iran war, near January peak. Will AI boom, shortage propel red metal to new highs?
summary: Copper prices are surging due to geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand. The metal is increasingly viewed as a strategic commodity for the AI era, with markets anticipating a prolonged supply crunch due to underinvestment and long lead times for new mining projects[2].

title: Copper prices hit a new record high, but is the metals trade 'stretched'?
summary: Copper hit a fresh record high, while gold and silver trade near their highs. The metal continues to climb after breaking above $13,000 a ton for the first time[3].

title: Copper price falls on Grasberg production resumption
summary: Freeport McMoRan has restarted copper concentrate supply to a domestic smelter in Indonesia, though the export ban on concentrates from its iconic Grasberg mine remains in place. Reuters reports that staged production resumed following a 38-day hiatus caused by a strike at a domestic refinery where the US company holds a minority stake.

title: Development of $9bn copper and gold project pushed back
summary: A major $9 billion copper and gold project has been delayed as the ongoing Middle East war exacerbates worsening local security conditions. This development adds to capacity constraints in the global copper market amid rising demand.

title: Anglo American and Codelco complete Los Bronces - Andina agreement
summary: Anglo American and Codelco have finalized an agreement for the Los Bronces - Andina project, paving the way to deliver an additional 2.7 million tons of copper production. This capacity increase is expected to help address current supply deficits in the global market.

title: Copper producers report higher earnings in Q1 2026
summary: Copper producers, including Atlas Mining, reported elevated earnings in the first quarter of 2026 driven by high copper prices, though concentrate output remains under pressure. The report highlights ongoing challenges in securing adequate production capacity amid global supply disruptions.

title: Record Copper Prices Signal Supply Race
summary: Analysts at Citi estimate refined copper production will reach 26.9 million tons this year, indicating a deficit of 308,000 tons. Record prices are driving increased investments in new copper capacity to close the supply gap.

title: Nickel Prices Today 2026 | Live Chart, Battery Grade & Stainless Steel
summary: Global nickel prices dropped 2.05% to $16,839.98 per ton on June 25, 2026, with Chinese markets reflecting a parallel decline. Nickel closed lower on June 27, 2026 at $16,785.64 per ton globally, marking a -0.32% change.

title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
summary: Indonesia's strategic shift represents a significant development in nickel markets, challenging previous pessimistic forecasts. The global nickel market is experiencing a critical inflection point as supply and demand dynamics converge to potentially end prolonged range-bound pricing between $15,000-$15,800 per ton since April 2025.

title: Nickel Futures Price Today
summary: The current price of Nickel futures is 16,795.63, with a previous close of 16,860.88. The daily trading range for Nickel futures reflects this recent price movement.

title: Nickel fell to $16,700 per tonne, nearing its lowest level since late December
summary: Nickel prices fell to $16,700 per tonne, approaching levels seen in late December, as Indonesia prepares to permit a substantial increase in nickel mine output. This development adds pressure to the market amid ongoing supply-demand dynamics.

title: Nickel Prices Off The Bottom
summary: Nickel prices have broken through US$10,000/t, with a new monthly report by SNL Metals & Mining expressing positive outlook on the market. This marks a significant recovery from previous lows.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has notified PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, to drastically cut its ore production quota from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons in 2026 to boost global prices. The company plans to apply for a revision of this decision, which has already caused nickel prices to climb last month.

title: Indonesian smelters cut production on lower nickel ore quota
summary: Indonesia's energy ministry reduced the 2026 nickel ore production quota to 260–270 million metric tons, below the 320 million tons produced last year and the industry's demand estimate of 340–350 million tons. This cut aims to address previous oversupply and depressed prices, leading Indonesian smelters to reduce their production output.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: On December 19, Indonesia's Minister of Energy and Mineral Resources confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue while cracking down on environmentally harmful operations. The country is reversing its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility and navigate the current nickel market.

title: Aosta, Cogne Acciai Speciali: bilancio in rosso e futuro incerto
summary: Cogne Acciai Speciali ha registrato una perdita di 60,9 milioni di euro nel 2025, in netto peggioramento rispetto all'utile di 8 milioni dell'anno precedente. L'azienda affronta una situazione complessa dovuta a dazi statunitensi, concorrenza asiatica e rincari dell'energia, con preoccupazioni da parte di CGIL e Fiom per le prospettive future.

summary: La cassa integrazione torna in Cogne Acciai Speciali, coinvolgendo 750 dipendenti per 13 settimane dal 9 giugno al 7 settembre 2026. Questa misura riflette le difficoltà economiche attuali dell'azienda nel settore dell'acciaio inossidabile e delle leghe di nichel.
